spaSim3D 0.1.0
  • Reference
  • Articles
    • Simulate with classic functions

    • Simulate with metadata functions

Simulate a network cluster with a ring in spaSim3D.

Source: R/simulate_network_ring.R
simulate_network_ring.Rd

This functions simulate a network cluster with a ring onto an existing SpatialExperiment object. The parameters of the network and its ring are fully customisable by the user.

simulate_network_ring(spe, ring_properties)

Arguments

spe

A SpatialExperiment object containing 3D spatial information for the cells. Should be generated using the output of one of the following functions: simulate_random_background_cells3D, simulate_ordered_background_cells3D, simulate_mixing3D or any of the other simulate_* functions. This is because the metadata of the SpatialExperiment object needs to already contain spaSim3D specific data relating to the background of the SpatialExperiment object, and any clusters.

ring_properties

A list containing the properties of the network cluster and ring desired. The list should contain the following elements:

"shape": Must be equal to the character "network".

"cluster_cell_types": A character vector representing the cell types that
    make up the cluster. E.g. c("Tumour", "Immune").

"cluster_cell_proportions": A numeric vector representing the proportion
    of each cell type in the cluster. Its elements must each be
    greater than 0, sum to 1 and the vector must be the same length as
    "cluster_cell_types". E.g. c(0.6, 0.4) corresponds to a cluster made
    up of 60% Tumour and 40% Immune.

"n_edges": A positive number capped at 99 representing the number of
    edges.

"width": A positive number representing the width of each network edge.

"centre_loc": A numerical vector of length 3 representing the approximate
    centre x,y,z coordinate of the network E.g. (0, 0, 0).

"radius": A positive number representing the rough radius spanned by the
    network.

"ring_cell_types": A character vector representing the cell types
    that make up the ring. E.g. c("Immune1", "Immune2", "Immune3").

"ring_cell_proportions": A numeric vector representing the
    proportion of each cell type in the ring. Its elements must each be
    greater than 0, sum to 1 and the vector must be the same length as
    "ring_cell_types". E.g. c(0.3, 0.4, 0.3) corresponds to an
    ring made up of 30% Immune1, 40% Immune2 and 30% Immune3.

"ring_width": A positive number representing the width of the ring.

Value

The same 3D SpatialExperiment object used as input for spe, updated with the new network with ring cluster and the corresponding metadata.

Examples

# Simulate background
bg_r <- simulate_random_background_cells3D(n_cells = 10000,
                                           length = 100,
                                           width = 100,
                                           height = 100,
                                           minimum_distance_between_cells = 0.5,
                                           background_cell_type = "Others",
                                           plot_image = FALSE)

# Simulate cluster
network_ring_cluster <- simulate_network_ring(bg_r,
                                              ring_properties = list(
                                                shape = "network",
                                                cluster_cell_types = c("Immune1"),
                                                cluster_cell_proportions = c(1),
                                                n_edges = 15,
                                                width = 8,
                                                centre_loc = c(50, 50, 50),
                                                radius = 50,
                                                ring_cell_types = c("Immune2"),
                                                ring_cell_proportions = c(1),
                                                ring_width = 2
                                              ))
# Plot
plot_cells3D(network_ring_cluster)
#> Warning: plot_cell_types not specified, all cell types found in the spe object will be used.
#> Warning: plot_colours not specified, viridis (D) palette will be used.
